_note: Solution Landscape canvas dataset. Each scene is a real node-graph architecture diagram: groups (regions), nodes (typed: system/legacy/agent/review/service/data/gateway/module/stage/infra/human/future/approach), and edges (kind: flow/dep/data/ctrl, optional label, optional dir 'h'|'v' to force the connection axis, optional animated for a flowing dot). Node x,y are top-left in a 1280x820 space; the engine fits and lets the reader drag/pan/zoom. title/blurb/narrative carry {en,fr}; node labels and detail are plain strings and read in both locales until translated. The narrative is the accessible description and the audio script. Downloadable, editable source for the canvas in paper xs7uh; scenes embed individually via canvas:scene.

blurb.en: Agentic reconnaissance reads the whole GitHub estate, one repository at a time, and reports the ground truth.
blurb.fr: Une reconnaissance agentique lit tout le parc GitHub, un dépôt à la fois.

narrative.en: This scene shows the Government of Alberta's legacy software estate being read by Git Insights. On the left, the GitHub estate: about four hundred sixty-six million lines of code across roughly three thousand four hundred repositories, with eight thousand contributors and nearly nine hundred fifty thousand commits over eight years. A two-tier scan reads it: deterministic code clones and counts each repository and finds known-bad patterns, then an AI judgment pass grades health and must cite an exact file and line. About fifty agents read the whole estate in roughly twenty hours for less than two thousand dollars. On the right, the outputs: health scores averaging three point two out of ten, a business-capability canvas, and a disposition for each system, keep, invest, migrate, or retire.
narrative.fr: TODO-FR: The legacy estate read by Git Insights: a deterministic scan and an AI judgment pass grade ~3,400 repositories, producing health scores, a capability canvas, and a disposition.

nodes.label: GitHub estate
nodes.detail: The whole legacy estate: 466,792,649 lines of code across ~3,400 repositories, 8,178 contributors, 948,571 commits, 145,486 pull requests, read one repository at a time.

nodes.label: Deterministic scan
nodes.detail: Lists files, frameworks, and dependencies and flags known-bad patterns. Same input, same output. Feeds the judgment pass.

nodes.label: AI judgment
nodes.detail: An AI pass that grades each repository out of ten across quality, architecture, security, tests, docs and dependencies, and must cite the exact file and line behind every judgment.

nodes.label: Health scores
nodes.detail: Per-repository health out of ten, rolled up into a nine-box active-versus-healthy grid. The estate averages 3.2 out of 10.

nodes.label: Capability canvas
nodes.detail: What each system actually does, reverse-engineered into a business-capability canvas with each conclusion traced to a source file.

nodes.label: Disposition
nodes.detail: A classification and disposition for every system, plus a modernization-effort estimate.

blurb.en: Four ways to modernize, climbing from lightest touch to the most complete rebuild. A real estate needs all four.
blurb.fr: Quatre façons de moderniser, de la plus légère à la reconstruction la plus complète.

narrative.en: This scene lays out the four approaches to modernization, climbing in ambition from left to right. Approach one, the AI Garage, fixes one system at a time in place. Approach two, the AI Factory, builds a fresh modular application from scratch with a team of specialist agents. Approach three, AI Rationalization, steps back to a whole ministry and collapses a portfolio of overlapping systems into a few shared modern modules, for example one hundred eighty-five applications into sixteen. Approach four, Government three point zero, dissolves the application itself: agents work directly against governed data and generate the interface a person needs in the moment. All four reach for about a ninety-five percent reduction in the cost and time of building and maintaining government software.
narrative.fr: TODO-FR: Four approaches climbing from the AI Garage to Government 3.0, all reaching for about a ninety-five percent reduction in cost and time.

nodes.label: AI Garage
nodes.detail: Repairs, remediates, or matches one system at a time, in place. Lowest risk; treats symptoms rather than the disease.

nodes.label: AI Factory
nodes.detail: Builds a fresh application from scratch with a team of specialist agents. Modular, government-owned, parts none much larger than ~1,000 lines.

nodes.label: AI Rationalization
nodes.detail: Steps back to a whole ministry and collapses a portfolio of overlapping systems into a few shared modern modules. A program that once ran 5 to 8 years can run in weeks to months.

nodes.label: Government 3.0
nodes.detail: Dissolves the application itself. AI agents work directly against governed data and generate the interface a person needs in the moment. Most radical, fastest, most cost-effective.

nodes.label: ≈95% less cost & time
nodes.sub: the prize all four reach for
nodes.detail: Each approach reaches for about a 95% reduction in the cost and time of building and maintaining government software.

blurb.en: The right model, in the right environment, for the right use case, with abstraction layers that prevent lock-in.
blurb.fr: Le bon modèle, dans le bon environnement, pour le bon cas d'usage.

narrative.en: This scene stacks the agentic technology platform in layers, from the cloud up to security. At the base sit cloud and compute: Google Cloud, Azure, AWS, an on-premises GPU cluster, and three legacy data centres. Above that is model access through AWS Bedrock, the Google Agent Platform, and Azure AI Studio. Then the models themselves, Claude Opus and Sonnet, with open-source models available offline. Above the models sits the Bifrost gateway, which handles abstraction, cost control, and personal-information protection. Then deployment, sending agentic workloads to Google Cloud and traditional applications to Azure and AWS. At the top, security and data-loss prevention through Microsoft Defender and Purview. The supply chain is diversified on purpose so no single provider can lock the province in.
narrative.fr: TODO-FR: The agentic stack in layers from cloud and compute up to security, with the Bifrost gateway preventing lock-in.

nodes.label: Security & data-loss prevention
nodes.detail: Defender and Purview plus GitHub tooling. ~99%+ of systems sit at Protected B at the highest.

nodes.label: Deployment
nodes.sub: agentic → Google Cloud · apps → Azure, AWS
nodes.detail: Agentic workloads deploy to Google Cloud; traditional applications to Azure and AWS.

nodes.label: Bifrost gateway
nodes.detail: The abstraction layer. Routes to the right model, controls cost and PII, and prevents provider lock-in.

nodes.label: Models
nodes.detail: Claude Opus and Sonnet for most work; open-source models available offline. 18 months of model trials across Gemini, OpenAI, and Anthropic.

nodes.label: Model access
nodes.sub: Bedrock · Google Agent Platform · Azure AI Studio
nodes.detail: Model access through AWS Bedrock, the Google Agent Platform, and Azure AI Studio. Enterprise agreements run 25 to 50 million tokens per minute.

nodes.label: Cloud & compute
nodes.sub: GCP · Azure · AWS · on-prem GPU · 3 data centres
nodes.detail: Google Cloud, Azure, AWS, an on-premises GPU cluster, and three legacy data centres.

title.en: The harness and its review agents
title.fr: Le harnais et ses agents de revue

blurb.en: A worker agent ringed by four review agents that gate the work: green, yellow, red, and blue.
blurb.fr: Un agent ouvrier entouré de quatre agents de revue : vert, jaune, rouge et bleu.

narrative.en: This scene shows a worker agent at the centre, gated by the four review agents that ship with every harness. Green checks code hygiene and the supply chain, and is deterministic. Yellow audits how the writing reads, catching AI tells, and is deterministic. Red plays the attacker, running outside-in reconnaissance and then planning concrete attack paths. Blue plays the defender, building a threat model and walking every OWASP control to write the ranked verdict. Feeding the worker from below are the parts of the harness itself: the rules file, the skills, the templates, the hooks, and the evals. A piece of work is not done until it passes the gates, and about ninety-five security controls are checked on every pass.
narrative.fr: TODO-FR: A worker agent gated by four review agents, green, yellow, red, blue, fed by the harness parts.

nodes.label: Worker agent
nodes.detail: The coding agent paired with a person. Its work is gated by the four review agents and grounded by the harness parts.

nodes.label: Green
nodes.detail: Vulnerable dependencies, committed secrets, dangerous patterns, thin coverage. ~30 deterministic checks. Runs first; the evidence base others read.

nodes.label: Yellow
nodes.detail: Walks 12 rules across all project prose, flagging AI tells with a plain rewrite. Deterministic, runs in parallel, cheapest.

nodes.label: Red
nodes.detail: Outside-in reconnaissance with deterministic tooling, then a Claude pass that plans concrete attack paths. Authorized targets only.

nodes.label: Blue
nodes.detail: Threat model, every OWASP ASVS Level 2 requirement walked, Red's recon folded into kill chains, then a ranked evidence-backed verdict.

nodes.label: CLAUDE.md
nodes.detail: The one-page rules file the agent reads first every session.

nodes.label: Skills
nodes.detail: Small playbooks in .claude/skills, opened only when the task matches.

nodes.label: Templates
nodes.detail: The masterpiece reference app: Vue + Vite, Node + Express, Postgres 18, SSO, with 100+ decisions pre-made.

nodes.label: Hooks
nodes.detail: Deterministic programs that run at key moments and cannot be talked out of a finding.

nodes.label: Evals
nodes.detail: Probabilistic and deterministic checks run on every release.

blurb.en: The siege is intensifying and the walls are aging. AI defends at machine speed, and modernization cuts the attack surface.
blurb.fr: Le siège s'intensifie et les murs vieillissent. L'IA défend à la vitesse de la machine.

narrative.en: This scene shows the cyber imperative: why modernization is a security requirement. On the left, the siege outside the wall. Cyber controls now block about one hundred eighty-nine million connection attempts a day, more than double two years ago; AI is generating exploits at machine speed; and the software supply chain has become the primary target. In the centre, the harness's review agents defend at the same speed: Red runs attacker reconnaissance and plans attack paths, Green checks the supply chain for vulnerable dependencies and secrets, and Blue, the defender, walks every OWASP control, folds Red's reconnaissance into kill chains, and writes the verdict, about ninety-five controls on every pass. On the right, the stakes and the move: an aging estate running past vendor support is the exposure, a single incident costs on the order of five million dollars a month, and modernizing into shared modules cuts the attack surface by more than ninety percent.
narrative.fr: TODO-FR: The cyber imperative: ~189M attempts blocked a day, AI-speed exploits, the supply chain as the target; Red/Green/Blue defend; modernization cuts the attack surface.

nodes.label: Connection attempts
nodes.detail: Cyber controls block about 189 million connection attempts a day, more than double two years ago.

nodes.label: AI-generated exploits
nodes.detail: Attackers use AI to generate exploits at lightning speed.

nodes.label: Supply-chain attacks
nodes.detail: The software supply chain has become a primary target.

nodes.label: Red — attacker sim
nodes.detail: Outside-in reconnaissance, then plans concrete attack paths. Authorized targets only.

nodes.label: Blue — defender
nodes.detail: Threat model, every OWASP ASVS Level 2 control, Red's recon folded into kill chains, then a ranked verdict.

nodes.label: Green — supply chain
nodes.detail: Vulnerable dependencies, committed secrets, dangerous patterns.

nodes.label: Aging estate
nodes.detail: Much of the estate runs on technology moving past vendor support. This is the exposure.

nodes.label: Modernized modules
nodes.detail: Modernizing into shared modules cuts complexity and attack surface by more than 90%.

nodes.label: Cost of one incident
nodes.detail: A single government cyber incident runs on the order of $5M per month, and that is close to a best case.

blurb.en: Pronghorn ideation, then Nexus orchestration, then Velocity delivery. Agents flow across the line.
blurb.fr: Idéation Pronghorn, puis orchestration Nexus, puis livraison Velocity.

narrative.en: This scene draws the AI factory as a pipeline in three stages, with agents flowing left to right. The first stage is Pronghorn, where ideation turns a ministry's requirements into a live prototype, a four-level requirements map, and an architecture canvas. The second stage is Nexus, where each developer gets a secure cloud sandbox running many agent instances; work is containerized and published to Google Cloud behind a private endpoint, with every action observable and audited through the Bifrost gateway. The third stage is Velocity, where delivery is measured on a game board and enforced by the Reaper, with a leaderboard. Agents move through the line as design agents, build agents, oversight agents, and architect agents, while a human and the agents hand off turns.
narrative.fr: TODO-FR: The AI factory: Pronghorn ideation, Nexus orchestration, Velocity delivery, agents flowing across.

nodes.label: Chat
nodes.detail: A live prototype in the browser, from an idea to something you can click.

nodes.label: Requirements
nodes.detail: A four-level requirements map: Epic, Feature, User Story, Acceptance Criteria.

nodes.label: Architecture canvas
nodes.detail: The architecture drawn as nodes and edges that human and agents both author, then handed to the build.

nodes.label: Developer VM
nodes.detail: Each developer gets a secure Google Cloud sandbox running unlimited agent instances, every action observable.

nodes.label: Containerize → GCloud
nodes.detail: Publish-this-app containerizes and deploys to Google Cloud in minutes, behind a private endpoint.

nodes.label: Observe & audit
nodes.detail: Every action observed and audited through the Bifrost gateway; agents even audit other agents.

nodes.label: Velocity game board
nodes.detail: Delivery measured on a snakes-and-ladders board: forward earns points, backsliding loses them.

nodes.label: The Reaper
nodes.detail: An enforcement agent that removes points when work slides back, at a 2x multiplier.

nodes.label: Leaderboard & audit
nodes.detail: A leaderboard and audit reports; a chess clock splits human and AI time.

blurb.en: Eight stages. Forward motion earns points; backsliding loses them permanently. The Reaper enforces.
blurb.fr: Huit étapes. Avancer rapporte des points ; reculer en coûte.

narrative.en: This scene is the Velocity delivery game, a snakes-and-ladders track of eight stages: requirements, planning, architecture, prototype, development, user testing, user acceptance, and deployment. A project token moves along the track. Forward motion earns points: starting a step earns ten, submitting for review earns twenty, completing a step earns one hundred. Backsliding loses them and the loss is permanent: a rejection costs thirty, sending work back a stage costs fifty per stage, reopening a completed step costs fifty. An enforcement agent called the Reaper removes the points. The board exists because old-style estimation fails once AI codes a hundred times faster, and the program target is a twentyfold acceleration of real delivery.
narrative.fr: TODO-FR: The Velocity board: eight stages, points for forward motion, permanent loss for backsliding, enforced by the Reaper, targeting a twentyfold acceleration.

nodes.label: Requirements
nodes.detail: Stage 1. Skills are organized one per stage; tracking is at the step level within a module. Start +10, submit +20, complete +100.

nodes.label: Planning
nodes.detail: Stage 2.

nodes.label: Architecture
nodes.detail: Stage 3.

nodes.label: Prototype
nodes.detail: Stage 4.

nodes.label: Development
nodes.detail: Stage 5.

nodes.label: User Testing
nodes.detail: Stage 6.

nodes.label: User Acceptance
nodes.detail: Stage 7.

nodes.label: Deployment
nodes.detail: Stage 8. Projects auto-create a workspace with a folder per module.

nodes.label: Scoring
nodes.detail: Start +10, submit +20, complete +100; reject −30, send back −50 per stage, reopen −50. The program target is a 20x acceleration of real delivery.

nodes.label: The Reaper
nodes.detail: The enforcement agent. Removes points when work backslides, and removes them again at a 2x multiplier.

blurb.en: Observer harnesses watch a builder from outside, file tickets into a filesystem bus, and the builder clears them.
blurb.fr: Des harnais observateurs surveillent un bâtisseur de l'extérieur et déposent des billets.

narrative.en: This scene shows the anti-drift harness keeping a long build in sync. A builder agent writes features and edits the chain of related artifacts, the fourteen links that run from requirements through schema, code, tests, and documentation. As a codebase grows, those links drift out of sync. Observer harnesses run as separate sessions outside the project and watch the chain: the anti-drift harness for chain drift, a security harness for vulnerabilities and secrets, a docs harness for stale documentation. When an observer finds a problem it drops a ticket into a filesystem bus, the tickets folder, and the builder reads those tickets at the start of each turn and clears them like any other work. The bus is just the filesystem, so a failure in one observer never breaks the builder.
narrative.fr: TODO-FR: Observer harnesses watch the builder's 14-link chain from outside and file tickets into a filesystem bus the builder clears each turn.

nodes.label: Builder
nodes.detail: Writes application code and edits the chain. Reads tickets at the top of each turn and clears them like any other work.

nodes.label: Chain of artifacts
nodes.detail: Requirements, schema, migrations, controllers, code, docs, specs, training — the chain that drifts out of sync as the codebase grows.

nodes.label: Anti-Drift harness
nodes.detail: Watches the chain across the fourteen links, reads deterministic evidence, and files a drift ticket when a link falls out of sync.

nodes.label: Security harness
nodes.detail: Watches for vulnerabilities, secrets, and dependency risk on every change.

nodes.label: Docs harness
nodes.detail: Watches the README, OpenAPI, and training docs against the code and flags stale documentation.

nodes.label: Tickets bus
nodes.detail: A passive filesystem inbox: observers append a markdown ticket, the builder consumes it. Crash-isolated; no observer shares context with the builder.

blurb.en: In an agentic hierarchy the ratio inverts: for every worker, many supervisors auditing the work.
blurb.fr: Dans une hiérarchie agentique, le ratio s'inverse.

narrative.en: This scene shows the inverted hierarchy from the compression problem. In a human organization there is roughly one manager for every eight staff. In an agentic hierarchy the ratio inverts: for every worker agent, you can reasonably run eight supervisor agents auditing and validating the work. The worker sits at the base, and above it sit the supervisors, each holding one concern: cybersecurity, quality assurance, change management, code review, infrastructure, communications, legal, and privacy. There can be one chef in the kitchen, surrounded by sub-agents each running its own continuous audit. The spatial picture is the point: the work of overseeing now outnumbers the work of producing, eight to one.
narrative.fr: TODO-FR: The inverted hierarchy: one worker agent, eight supervisor agents above it, each holding one concern.

nodes.label: Cybersecurity
nodes.detail: Audits security continuously.

nodes.label: QA
nodes.detail: Validates quality.

nodes.label: Change mgmt
nodes.detail: Tracks and manages change.

nodes.label: Code review
nodes.detail: Reviews the worker's code with a fresh context.

nodes.label: Infrastructure
nodes.detail: Watches infrastructure and deployment.

nodes.label: Communications
nodes.detail: Handles communications.

nodes.label: Legal
nodes.detail: Checks legal constraints.

nodes.label: Privacy
nodes.detail: Checks privacy and PII.

nodes.label: Worker agent
nodes.detail: The single producing agent. For every worker, eight supervisors audit and validate. The ratio inverts the human one-to-eight.

blurb.en: A ministry's overlapping systems collapse into a few shared modules, built once, with no redundancy.
blurb.fr: Les systèmes redondants d'un ministère se réduisent à quelques modules partagés.

narrative.en: This scene shows the future state, where a ministry's tangle of overlapping systems collapses into a small set of shared, government-owned modules. In one Alberta ministry, one hundred eighty-five applications reduce to sixteen reusable modules, each built once with no redundancy, and complexity and attack surface fall by more than ninety percent. An AI rationalization pass reads the whole ministry at once, reverse-engineers what every system does, and clusters the findings into business capabilities. The shared services that emerge include payments, geospatial mapping, contract management, identity and access, build and release and observability, and an integration fabric. On the left, the overlapping legacy systems; on the right, the few modern modules they fold into.
narrative.fr: TODO-FR: The future state: 185 applications collapse into 16 shared modules, complexity down more than ninety percent.

blurb.en: No application in the middle. Agents act on governed data through guardrails and generate the interface on demand.
blurb.fr: Aucune application au milieu. Les agents agissent sur des données gouvernées et génèrent l'interface à la demande.

narrative.en: This scene shows Government three point zero at runtime, the most radical of the four approaches. There is no application in the middle. A citizen or staff member asks for a service. An orchestrator agent interprets the request and works through the Bifrost guardrail layer, which enforces personal-information protection, access control, and an audit trail. It reads and writes the governed data, the single source of truth, and checks the request against policy, the legislation and regulation that govern it. Then it generates the interface the person needs in the moment, and disposes of it afterward. The application as a fixed artifact dissolves; what remains is governed data, policy, and agents that compose the experience on demand.
narrative.fr: TODO-FR: Government 3.0 at runtime: a request to an orchestrator agent, through Bifrost guardrails, against governed data and policy, generating the interface on demand with no fixed app.

nodes.label: Citizen / staff
nodes.detail: A person asks for a service in plain terms, with no app to learn.

nodes.label: Orchestrator agent
nodes.detail: Interprets the request and composes the response from governed data and policy.

nodes.label: Policy & rules
nodes.detail: The legislation and regulation that govern the request; the model stays synchronized to it.

nodes.label: Bifrost guardrails
nodes.detail: Enforces personal-information protection, access control, and an audit trail on every action.

nodes.label: Governed data
nodes.detail: The governed data, read and written only through the guardrail layer.

nodes.label: Generated interface
nodes.sub: built in the moment, then disposed
nodes.detail: The interface the person needs, generated on demand and disposed afterward. No fixed application as an artifact.
